title: NOC 82010 – Supervisors, logging and forestry | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 82010 are the operational leaders of Canada’s vast forestry and logging industries. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals bridge the gap between high-level management and field crews, coordinating everything from timber harvesting to vital silviculture (reforestation) activities. In 2026, as Canada intensifies its efforts toward sustainable forest management and wildfire resilience, these supervisors are in high demand to ensure environmental compliance and crew safety. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for Express Entry (Category-Based Selection) and various Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) in resource-rich provinces.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level2Major Group82 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group820 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8201 – Supervisors, logging and forestryNOC Code82010

duties: Supervise and coordinate the activities of logging and forestry workers in woodland operations across multiple work sites.
Oversee silvicultural activities including scarification, tree planting, and vegetation control.
Schedule work crews, heavy equipment, and transportation to optimize production and meet deadlines.
Conduct safety briefings, recognize unsafe work conditions, and enforce strict occupational health and safety (OHS) regulations.
Communicate with technical and management personnel regarding forest management plans and harvesting procedures.
Resolve work-related problems and recommend measures to improve operational efficiency.
Prepare detailed production reports and oversee the hiring and training of new workers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Completion of a 1- to 3-year college program for forestry technologists or technicians may be required by some employers.
Work Experience: Several years of experience as a logger, silvicultural worker, or logging machinery operator are usually mandatory.
Certifications: An Industrial First Aid certificate and a Chemical Application license (for silviculture) are often required.
Strong leadership, communication, and project management skills are essential for managing remote crews.
salary_prospects: Note: Total compensation in remote "Camp Jobs" can include significant overtime and living allowances.
job_outlook: The outlook is Balanced to Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), a total of 1,700 job openings are projected through 2033, primarily driven by replacement demand due to retirements. The sector's shift toward high-tech forest management is creating a specific niche for supervisors who can bridge traditional logging skills with digital proficiency.
labour_market_demand: The demand for logging and forestry supervisors is Favourable, particularly in the western and Atlantic provinces. In 2026, the industry is seeing a steady need for supervisors to manage "Smart Logging" operations that utilize GPS and drone technology for precision harvesting. Significant opportunities exist in British Columbia, New Brunswick, and Ontario, where the forestry sector remains a primary economic driver and is currently facing a moderate wave of retirements among senior field staff.
Sustainable Leadership: Work in one of the world’s most advanced forestry sectors, leading the way in sustainable harvesting and land reclamation.
Competitive Compensation: As a TEER 2 supervisory role, salaries are significantly higher than entry-level field positions, often including remote work premiums.
Technological Advancement: Manage crews using modern LiDAR mapping, automated harvesters, and real-time operational software.
Direct Path to PR: Highly valued in the Federal Skilled Trades Program and the Express Entry category-based draws for Trade occupations.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted for candidates with experience in "Trade" or "Agriculture/Natural Resources" categories.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): An excellent pathway for supervisors with the required work experience and a job offer.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting the province's vital forest products sector.
Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): Targeted via the Foreign Worker or Human Capital streams for skilled supervisors.
